county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Glades County Clerk (Moore Haven). Formed 1921 from DeSoto. Pre-1921 records in DeSoto (and earlier in Manatee). Lake Okeechobee area. Moore Haven was devastated by the 1926 hurricane. Brighton Seminole Reservation is within the county. Glades County Historical Society has limited materials. Cattle ranching and sugar cane industry records. Very sparse population before 20th-century drainage projects.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1925; 20th-century formation; Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Gulf County Clerk (Port St. Joe). Formed 1925 from Calhoun — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1925 records in Calhoun (and earlier in Jackson and Franklin). Port St. Joe was the site of Florida's first constitutional convention (1838). Constitution Convention Museum. Gulf County Historical Society has local materials. St. Joseph Bay area. Timber and turpentine industry records. Cape San Blas area settlement.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1895 courthouse fire destroyed many early records including deeds and court minutes
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1827; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Hamilton County Clerk (Jasper). Formed 1827 from Jefferson during the territorial period. BURNED COUNTY — 1895 courthouse fire destroyed many early records including deeds and court minutes. Pre-1827 records in Jefferson (and earlier in Leon). Check Florida State Archives for surviving state copies and tax rolls. Suwannee River area. Hamilton County Historical Society has limited reconstructed materials. Stephen Foster Memorial at White Springs. Tobacco and cotton plantation county with significant enslaved population.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Hardee County Clerk (Wauchula). Formed 1921 from DeSoto — a 20th-century county. Pre-1921 records in DeSoto (and earlier in Manatee). Peace River area cattle ranching heritage. Hardee County Historical Society has limited materials. Phosphate mining records important for 20th-century families. Named for Cary Hardee, Florida governor. Wauchula area citrus and agricultural records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1923; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Hendry County Clerk (LaBelle). Formed 1923 from Lee — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1923 records in Lee (and earlier in Monroe). Caloosahatchee River area. Big Cypress Seminole Reservation partially in the county. LaBelle Heritage Museum. Sugar cane and cattle ranching industry records. Very sparse population before 20th-century drainage. Named for Captain Francis Asbury Hendry, a Florida cattleman and legislator.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1843; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Hernando County Clerk (Brooksville). Formed 1843 from Alachua during the territorial period. Originally named Benton County, renamed Hernando 1850. Pre-1843 records in Alachua. Parent county for Citrus (1887) and Pasco (1887). Brooksville area pioneer families. Hernando Historical Museum Association has local collections. Chinsegut Hill Manor House records. Withlacoochee River area settlement.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Highlands County Clerk (Sebring). Formed 1921 from DeSoto — a 20th-century county. Pre-1921 records in DeSoto (and earlier in Manatee). Lake Placid and Sebring area. Highlands Hammock State Park. Citrus and cattle ranching heritage. Highlands County Historical Society has local materials. Sebring was founded by ceramics manufacturer George Sebring in 1912. Archbold Biological Station records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1834; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Hillsborough County Clerk (Tampa). Formed 1834 from Alachua and Mosquito (now Orange). One of Florida's older and most populous counties. Tampa was a major cigar manufacturing center — Ybor City's Cuban, Italian, and Spanish immigrant records are exceptional. Tampa-Hillsborough County Public Library has extensive genealogical collections. Henry B. Plant Museum. Tampa Bay History Center. Fort Brooke military records at NARA. Significant African American community records. Port Tampa shipping records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1848; antebellum county; Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Holmes County Clerk (Bonifay). Formed 1848 from Walton and Jackson during the territorial period. Panhandle county with strong ties to Alabama. Pre-1848 records in Walton and Jackson. Holmes County Historical Society has local materials. Choctawhatchee River area. Many families migrated from Alabama and Georgia — cross-reference those state records. Farming and timber heritage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1925; 20th-century formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Indian River County Clerk (Vero Beach). Formed 1925 from St. Lucie — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1925 records in St. Lucie (and earlier in Brevard). Indian River citrus industry records. Pelican Island (first national wildlife refuge, 1903). Indian River County Historical Society has local materials. McKee Botanical Garden area. Barrier island settlement records. Very sparse population before the 20th century.
